notes: Refreshed for v1.12 (spec 44 Step 4d headless-mode fixes for gemini/copilot/codex). The pinned snyk-agent-scan==0.5.1 is non-deterministic for this skill: the package pins the client, not the scanner's server-side backend model, and across runs on this PR it has emitted W011+W012, then W007+W011 (4 consecutive runs + a CI run), then W007+W011+W012 (5 consecutive runs + a CI run). To keep the deterministic CI gate stable against this flapping, the baseline pins the SUPERSET of all observed findings — W007, W011, W012 — so any run that emits a subset reads as a cleared finding (an improvement, not a regression) rather than failing CI, while a genuinely new finding ID still trips the gate. None of the three is introduced by the v1.12 change; all are presence-based heuristics on features the skill needs: W011 (untrusted external command output ingested via `gh pr view`/`gh pr diff`), W012 (external-CLI handoff to copilot/codex/gemini), W007 (insecure credential handling — the review templates quote a phrase anchor from the diff and the self/claude path can return unredacted diff content). Mitigations (untrusted-content boundary markers, stdin transport for the bulk prompt on gemini/codex, the Step 4b pre-flight secret scan that detects secret patterns and requires explicit confirmation before any external-CLI handoff, argument validation) are documented in skills/peer-review/SKILL.md `## Security model`. The v1.12 change moved copilot's prompt to `-p` on argv (documented there as a residual risk backstopped by the secret scan); gemini and codex keep the untrusted bulk prompt on stdin (gemini carries only a short fixed directive on argv via `-p`).
